title: Connector binding entry (v2 — Facet C)
description: A single per-role connector grant as it appears in charter tooling.<role>.connectors[] (plan §4.1 Facet C + §5). Facet C is DEFAULT-DENY: a role may use ONLY the connectors explicitly granted here. Distinct from built-in `tools` (the existing whitelist) — connectors are MCP servers / HTTP APIs / CLIs. HARD RULES (Constitution §1.7 new item + §3.4 invariant #6): role grant ⊇ the connector requirements of the role's skills; connector capability class ⊆ role sandbox; secrets are referenced BY NAME only (values live in the adopter env, never the charter); MCP servers MUST be pinned + provenance-bearing (third-party = a trust decision). A scan is an authoring aid, NOT runtime authorization.

id.description: Connector id; MUST match a key in connectors/registry.yaml connectors{}.

kind.description: Connector mechanism. mcp = MCP server; http_api = REST/HTTP endpoint; cli = local command-line tool.

server.description: Server/endpoint reference, pinned: '<ref>@<pin>' for MCP (pin = version/commit/digest), base URL for http_api, command for cli. Unpinned/runtime-fetched sources are FORBIDDEN (Constitution §1.7 new item).

tools.description: OPTIONAL allowlist of the specific tool/function names exposed by this connector that the role may call. Omit ⇒ all tools the connector exposes (still bounded by scopes + sandbox). Narrowing here is encouraged (least privilege).

scopes.description: Capability classes this grant authorizes. MUST be ⊆ the role's sandbox (a read_only role cannot hold a write/network connector). Default-deny: a scope not listed is not granted.

items.description: Environment variable NAME only (UPPER_SNAKE_CASE). The value is resolved from the adopter env at runtime and MUST NOT appear in the charter.

secrets.description: Names of secrets the connector requires. By-name only — values stay in the adopter env.

provenance.description: Trust class of the connector source. Third-party MCP servers are an explicit trust decision.
